summary.intent: Address Codex review comments on PR #704 (issue-287-range-calendar-core) by pushing a fix commit, resolving the three open review threads, and shepherding the PR to full CI green and Codex approval.
summary.outcome: All three Codex review threads were replied to and resolved, Codex approved the PR, and a follow-up commit adding branch-coverage tests cleared the failing ars-components coverage gate (89.8% → 90.1%), leaving the PR in a fully green, approved state pending merge.

repo: ars-components has a 90% branch coverage minimum enforced by a workspace-level `cargo xcov` gate; falling below it fails the CI Coverage job.
The project uses a Codex bot reviewer triggered by posting `@codex review` as an issue comment; approval surfaces as a PR-level thumbs-up reaction.
Commits co-authored with Codex require the trailer `Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>`.

The RangeCalendar component validates external ranges through sanitize_external_range, which covers span constraints, date bounds, and unavailable-date predicates — new validation paths added in this PR introduced uncovered branches.
zero visible_months falls back to a documented two-month default on both init and prop sync.

code.finding: These line ranges in the llvm-cov text output correspond to the previously untested validation branches: unavailable-date predicates, min/single-date rejection, max-bound rejection, uncontrolled revalidation after prop constraints, and hover-only pending revalidation.

workflow: cargo llvm-cov per-module summary is NOT equivalent to the workspace xcov gate — always run `cargo xcov` to verify the same threshold CI checks.
GitHub Actions job log endpoints can transiently return 404 immediately after a job fails while GitHub finalizes the run; waiting 60s and retrying resolves it.
CI coverage and release verification jobs are the slowest tail of the matrix (300s+ after other checks pass); this is expected and not a signal of failure.
Filtering large CI logs with `rg -n` for ERROR/FAIL/threshold keywords is the fastest way to find the exact failure line in a multi-thousand-line coverage log.
Codex review threads must be explicitly resolved via the GraphQL resolveReviewThread mutation after replying — replies alone do not mark them resolved.

friction: GitHub Actions job log endpoint returned a transient 404 immediately after the coverage job failed, requiring a 60-second wait and retry before logs were accessible.
cargo llvm-cov per-module summary passed locally but did not match the workspace xcov threshold gate, causing confusion about whether coverage was actually fixed before running xcov.
Coverage and Release Verification jobs took significantly longer than the rest of the CI matrix, requiring multiple 240–300 second polling waits with no intermediate progress signal.

CI coverage failure was not immediately obvious from the job name alone — the Codecov upload succeeding in the log tail made it look like the upload was the failure, when the actual threshold check failed earlier in the same job.
open_items: Release Verification job status at session end is not explicitly confirmed green — the session ends after committing the coverage fix but before pushing and observing the final CI run outcome.
The PR is not yet merged; Codex approval and CI green are achieved but merge itself is deferred.
